_comment: Execution-proof request — IDENTICAL under Option A and Option B. The execution proof statement (§2.1) and its 15-field PI tuple do not change between topologies; only what consumes the proof afterward (aggregation in A, compression-as-aggregator in B) is different. Therefore one shape lives here and is referenced from both option directories.
_comment_publicInputs: The 15 fields from §2.1 + §6.4. Coordinator supplies prev_* values; the guest recomputes new_* from the witness and asserts equality. The L1 contract verifies prev_* against its stored anchors after aggregation+SNARK wrap.
_comment_chainConfig: Private input. The guest hashes (Keccak256 over RLP) and asserts the result equals publicInputs.dynamicChainConfigHash. Single value across the range — a config change is a finalization boundary, not a per-block event.
_comment_blocks: Full Ethereum block headers (RLP) plus signature-stripped tx list. fromAddresses are stored explicitly so the guest can avoid in-circuit ECDSA recovery — keccak256(concat(fromAddresses across blocks)) is what binds publicInputs.txFromsHash. The parent block (immediately before startBlockNumber) is *not* a top-level field — its state root and any other anchoring data are part of the executionWitness payload below; the guest verifies that anchor against publicInputs.prevLastBlockHash. L2→L1 message hashing is derived from re-execution alone (filter MessageSent logs from chainConfig.l2MessageServiceContract, accumulate, assert keccak256 == publicInputs.L2L1MessagesHash). L1→L2 rolling hash is the asymmetric case — see l1L2Messages below.

forcedTransactions._comment: Per §2.1 + §6.5, FTXs are processed *after* normal block execution and appear at the start of the block tx list; per-FTX state_witness is non-null ONLY when acceptance is ACCEPTED-but-invalid (§6.5 'Invalid' branch).

_comment_l1L2Messages: L1→L2 deposit messages consumed in this range. Each entry feeds one rolling-hash step; the guest chains them starting from publicInputs.prevL1L2BridgeRollingHash and asserts the final value equals publicInputs.L1L2BridgeRollingHash. messageNumber must be strictly increasing; the first entry's messageNumber must equal prevL1L2BridgeRollingHashMessageNumber + 1.
_comment_forcedTransactionContext: Witness data needed by the §6.5 deadline-sweep check. pendingFtxs lists every FTX with number > publicInputs.lastProcessedFtxNumber whose deadline ≤ some upper bound the coordinator picks (typically the last L1 block hash known at request time). The guest asserts: for every entry where deadlineBlockNumber ≤ publicInputs.newLastBlockNumber, ftxNumber must already be ≤ publicInputs.lastProcessedFtxNumber — i.e. nothing past-deadline was skipped. The L1 contract performs the symmetric check against its full stored ftxDeadline[] map at finalization time.
_comment_executionWitness: Stateless witness from Besu `debug_executionWitness`. Format follows EIP-7864 (Verkle) post-Glamsterdam, falling back to MPT proofs in the transitional period. The witness envelope carries the parent state root that its account/storage proofs are anchored to; the guest asserts that anchor is consistent with publicInputs.prevLastBlockHash, then replays the EVM directly. One entry per block; the parent state root anchor lives at the top of the witness, not inside any per-block entry. No separate state-manager preprocessing step is needed (the Type-2 zkStateMerkleProof + conflatedExecutionTracesFile pair is gone). Treated as opaque-but-deterministic by the coordinator: the JSON is forwarded as-is, the guest is the only consumer.

executionWitness._comment_parentStateAnchor: Top-level anchor; the guest checks it is consistent with publicInputs.prevLastBlockHash. Concrete shape (e.g. parent header RLP vs. parent state root + an inclusion proof against prevLastBlockHash) is pinned down by the prover team alongside the EIP-7864 witness format.
